prompt: Photorealistic architectural signage photography. A carved stone sign mounted on the facade of a modern commercial building in Adelaide, South Australia. The sign is a solid dark charcoal (#2C2C2C) parallelogram-shaped stone block with one corner chamfered at 45 degrees, forming a distinctive cornerstone/keystone shape. The letters LK are deeply carved and inset into the stone surface, filled with real gold leaf (#C9A96E), catching warm light. A thin gold line runs along the bottom edge of the stone block. Below the stone shape, the words DESIGN AND BUILD are carved in refined small capitals with generous letter-spacing. The sign is illuminated by warm architectural uplighting from below, creating dramatic shadows in the carved letterforms. Evening golden hour light, sandstone building facade behind. Shot on 85mm f/1.8 lens, shallow depth of field, cinematic colour grading, premium construction company branding, Adelaide CBD architecture.
